name: Boulevardier
description: A spirit-forward classic built on rye, Campari, and sweet vermouth—bold, bittersweet, and perfectly balanced for a dinner party.
steps: Add rye, Campari, sweet vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oil over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: A timeless aperitif cocktail that showcases your rye beautifully. The Campari and vermouth create complexity and depth—ideal for a dinner party where guests appreciate spirit-forward drinks.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, earthy take on the Negroni using Del Maguey Vida mezcal instead of gin, balanced with Campari and dry vermouth.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, dry vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oil over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: The mezcal's smoke and minerality play beautifully against Campari's bitter-sweet profile and the dry vermouth's herbal notes. A sophisticated, conversation-starting drink that feels special without being fussy.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A bracing, bitter aperitivo built on smoky mezcal, Campari's herbal bite, and dry vermouth—stirred ice-cold and served up. The smoke plays beautifully against the camphor and citrus notes.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and dry vermouth to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–12 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or Nick & Nora glass
Optional: express a lemon twist over the surface and discard, or add as garnish
why: This hits every mark: bitter, bracing, spirit-forward, and perfectly balanced without sweetness. The mezcal's smoke and minerality elevate the classic formula, and stirring keeps it elegant and aperitivo-appropriate. All bottles are on hand.

name: Bourbon & Orange Nightcap
description: A warming, simple drink that lets the bourbon shine with bright citrus notes—perfect for unwinding before bed.
steps: Gently warm a rocks glass with hot water, then empty it
Add sugar and a splash of water to the glass and stir to dissolve
Pour in the bourbon and orange juice
Stir well
Express the orange peel oils over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: Four Roses Small Batch has a smooth, slightly fruity profile that pairs beautifully with fresh orange. This simple format keeps the focus on the bourbon while the citrus and warmth make it an ideal nightcap—no complex techniques or missing ingredients required.

name: Tropical Bourbon Punch
description: A fruity, refreshing tiki-style punch built on Four Roses Small Batch bourbon with tropical juices and a touch of sweetness, perfect for a beach party.
steps: Fill a tiki mug or highball glass with ice
Pour 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch over the ice
Add 3 oz pineapple juice and 2 oz orange juice
Squeeze in 0.5 oz fresh lime juice
Stir in 0.5 oz simple syrup
Stir well to combine
Garnish with a pineapple wedge or orange slice if available
why: Four Roses Small Batch is a smooth, approachable bourbon with subtle fruit notes that pairs beautifully with tropical juices. This punch is easy to batch for a party, refreshing in the heat, and captures the tiki spirit without requiring specialty liqueurs you don't have on hand.

name: Four Roses Old Fashioned
description: A timeless whiskey cocktail with bourbon, sugar, bitters, and a twist of citrus—the gold standard for sipping bourbon neat or on the rocks.
steps: Place sugar and bitters in a rocks glass
Add a splash of water and stir to dissolve
Add ice
Pour in 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch
Stir well
Express orange peel oil over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: The Four Roses is smooth and approachable—perfect for a casual evening. An Old Fashioned lets the bourbon shine without fuss.

name: Bourbon Sour
description: A balanced whiskey sour with bourbon, fresh lemon juice, and a touch of sweetness—bright, refreshing, and easy to make.
steps: Fill a shaker with ice
Add 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch, 0.75 oz fresh lemon juice, and 0.5 oz simple syrup
Shake vigorously for 10 seconds
Strain into a rocks glass filled with fresh ice
Serve immediately
why: A classic sour is versatile and crowd-pleasing. The Four Roses' vanilla notes complement the citrus beautifully, and it's quick to make.

name: Bourbon Neat
description: Four Roses Small Batch served at room temperature or with a single large ice cube—the purest way to experience the bourbon's character.
steps: Pour 2 oz Four Roses Small Batch into a rocks glass
Add a single large ice cube or serve at room temperature
Optional: add a splash of water to open up the flavors
Sip and enjoy
why: Sometimes the best drink is the simplest. Four Roses Small Batch is well-balanced and approachable on its own—perfect for a relaxed evening when you want to taste what you're drinking.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, spirit-forward take on the classic Negroni, built with Del Maguey Vida mezcal for earthy complexity, Campari for bitter-herbal notes, and dry vermouth for structure. Perfectly balanced and intensely aromatic.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, dry vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–12 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop as garnish
why: This drink plays directly to your wife's preferences: mezcal-forward, spirit-driven, and built with dry vermouth to avoid sweetness. The smoke from the mezcal pairs beautifully with Campari's bitter complexity, creating an intimate, sophisticated date-night sipper with real depth.

name: Mezcal Paloma
description: A bright, citrusy tequila-style drink built on smoky mezcal with fresh lime juice, grapefruit, and a touch of orange liqueur for balance—perfect for taco night.
steps: Rim a highball glass with salt
Fill glass with ice
Pour mezcal, Cointreau, lime juice, grapefruit juice, and simple syrup into the glass
Stir well
Garnish with a lime wheel
why: Mezcal's smokiness pairs beautifully with tacos, while the bright citrus (lime and grapefruit) keeps the drink refreshing and summery. Cointreau adds subtle orange notes without overpowering the fresh flavors. This is a natural fit for the occasion and your bright, citrusy mood.

name: Boulevardier
description: A rich, warming cocktail with bourbon, sweet vermouth, and Campari—like a cozy negroni with deeper, more complex flavors. Perfect for settling in with a movie.
steps: Add bourbon, sweet vermouth, Campari, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for about 30 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express the orange peel oils over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: This is the ultimate cozy cocktail—spirit-forward, warming, and complex without being fussy. It's made entirely from bottles you own, requires no special ingredients, and the rich caramel and bitter-sweet notes are perfect for movie night relaxation.

name: Boulevardier
description: A classic aperitivo cocktail with bourbon, Campari, and sweet vermouth—rich, bitter, and perfectly balanced for the pre-dinner hour.
steps: Add bourbon, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10–15 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express the orange peel oils over the drink and use as garnish
why: You don't have gin, so a true Negroni isn't possible—but your bourbon, Campari, and vermouth make a Boulevardier, which is equally classic, equally bitter, and perfectly suited to aperitivo. It's a sophisticated, spirit-forward drink that showcases all three bottles you own.

name: Sazerac
description: A classic New Orleans rye whiskey cocktail with a rinse of absinthe, balanced with sugar and Angostura bitters for a bold, aromatic nightcap.
steps: Chill a rocks glass with ice and water, then discard and rinse with absinthe
In a mixing glass, muddle sugar with water and Angostura bitters
Add Rittenhouse Rye and ice, stir well for 30 seconds
Strain into the absinthe-rinsed glass
Express lemon peel oils over the drink and serve
why: You have the two essential spirits (rye and bitters) for an authentic Sazerac. The drink requires absinthe for its signature rinse—a specialty bottle worth acquiring for this classic.

name: Mai Tai
description: A classic tropical rum cocktail with a perfect balance of sweet, tart, and complex flavors. This version adapts the traditional recipe using your bourbon as the base spirit, creating a warm, oaky take on the iconic drink.
steps: Add bourbon, lime juice, simple syrup, orgeat, and orange curaçao to a cocktail shaker filled with ice
Shake vigorously for 10-12 seconds
Strain into a rocks glass filled with crushed ice
Garnish with a lime wheel and mint sprig
why: A Mai Tai traditionally requires light and dark rum, orgeat, and curaçao—bottles you don't currently own. Your Four Roses bourbon can substitute as the base spirit, though you'll need to source the orgeat and curaçao to complete this classic. The result is a bourbon-forward tropical drink perfect for a beach party vibe.

name: Four Roses Old Fashioned
description: A classic whiskey cocktail with bourbon, sugar, bitters, and a twist of citrus. Stirred down and served over ice.
steps: Place sugar and water in a rocks glass
Add bitters and stir to dissolve
Add bourbon and stir
Add ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: Four Roses Small Batch is perfect for a classic Old Fashioned—smooth, approachable, and lets the spirit shine. This is the gold standard whiskey cocktail for a relaxing evening.

name: Rittenhouse Sazerac
description: A New Orleans classic built on rye whiskey, absinthe rinse, and Peychaud's bitters with a lemon twist.
steps: Rinse a rocks glass with absinthe and discard excess
In a mixing glass, combine rye, bitters, sugar, and water with ice
Stir for 10 seconds
Strain into the absinthe-rinsed glass
Express lemon peel and drop in
why: Rittenhouse Rye is a high-proof, spicy rye that shines in this bold New Orleans drink. The Sazerac is sophisticated and warming—perfect for a casual evening that feels special.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky twist on the classic Negroni, swapping gin for mezcal. Bitter, herbal, and complex with a smoky edge.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, and sweet vermouth to a mixing glass with ice
Stir for 10 seconds
Strain into a rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel and drop in as garnish
why: Del Maguey Vida's bright, vegetal mezcal character plays beautifully against Campari's bitterness. This is a sophisticated sipper that surprises with smoke and complexity.

name: Four Roses Whiskey Smash
description: A refreshing bourbon cocktail with citrus and mint. Shaken and served up or over ice.
steps: Add bourbon, lemon juice, simple syrup, and mint to a cocktail shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10 seconds
Strain into a coupe glass or over fresh ice in a rocks glass
Garnish with a mint sprig
why: A bright, herbaceous take on bourbon that's approachable and delicious. The mint and citrus make it feel summery and fun—a great surprise for a casual evening.

name: Rittenhouse Sazerac-Style Sidecar
description: A rye-based sour with citrus and a touch of sweetness, shaken and served up.
steps: Add rye, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10 seconds
Strain into a coupe glass
Serve up
why: A clean, spirit-forward sour that lets Rittenhouse's spicy rye character shine. It's balanced, refreshing, and feels classic without being fussy.

name: Mezcal Negroni
description: A smoky, spirit-forward aperitif with mezcal, Campari, and dry vermouth—herbaceous and complex without cloying sweetness.
steps: Add mezcal, Campari, dry vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass with ice
Stir until well-chilled, about 30 seconds
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express orange peel oils over the drink and drop in as garnish
why: Alex loves smoky mezcal and dislikes sweet—this drink leans into smoke and herbaceous complexity with zero residual sweetness. It's a backyard-friendly sipper that showcases the mezcal.

name: Rye & Cointreau Sour
description: A bright, citrus-forward sour built on rye whiskey and Cointreau with a touch of sweetness and smooth body—no bitters needed.
steps: Add rye, Cointreau, lemon juice, and simple syrup to a cocktail shaker with ice
Shake vigorously for 10–12 seconds until very cold
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Serve immediately
why: Sam enjoys sweet and fruity flavors and dislikes bitter—this rye sour is balanced, citrusy, and approachable with no Campari or bitters. The Cointreau adds orange brightness and a touch of luxury to a backyard setting.

name: Boulevardier
description: A stirred aperitivo cocktail built on bourbon, Campari, and sweet vermouth—bold, bittersweet, and perfectly suited to aperitivo hour. The Campari's herbal bite and Carpano's vanilla warmth create a sophisticated, low-ABV sipper.
steps: Add bourbon, Campari, sweet vermouth, and bitters to a mixing glass filled with ice
Stir for 10–12 seconds until well-chilled
Strain into a coupe or rocks glass over fresh ice
Express an orange twist over the drink and drop it in as garnish
why: This is the quintessential aperitivo cocktail—bittersweet, spirit-forward, and designed to awaken the palate before a meal. It plays directly to Jamie's love of bitter and amaro notes while avoiding anything sweet or fruity.
